mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in housing starts signals contraction in residential investment and tighter mortgage-credit transmission, reducing expected cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST was 1191 thousand units as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of observations following negative 6-month HOUST trends, or if the HOUST trend coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, restrictive policy raises discount rates and refinancing stress faster than earnings expectations adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 4.76% as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% have average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment from low levels signals the transition from tight labor markets to weakening household income, compressing consumption expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 4.7% as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of rising UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS does not forecast lower 20-day returns than either variable alone, or if triggered returns are negative in less than 50% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales growth indicates slowing household demand and lower expected revenue growth for consumer-sensitive sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS was 380259 million dollars as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if observations after RSAFS 3-month annualized growth crosses below 0% do not have lower mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening real activity and deteriorating operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO was 114.357 as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO momentum combined with slowing GDPC1 does not produce lower 60-day returns than baseline, or if the interaction term has a nonnegative sign in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: An easing move in the federal funds rate after restrictive policy signals central-bank concern about growth and credit stress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite falls.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months while UNRATE is not falling
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 4.76% and UNRATE was 4.7% as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns following FEDFUNDS declines with non-improving UNRATE are positive in more than 60% of cases, or if results vanish after excluding recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll employment growth with unemployment below 5% signals resilient labor income and supports near-term consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is below 5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS was 138421 thousand persons and UNRATE was 4.7% as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns, or if the PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-below-5 interaction is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices during a restrictive-rate environment erode real household purchasing power and limit the Federal Reserve's ability to ease, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation is above 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL was 208.903 and FEDFUNDS was 4.76% as of the macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if high CPIAUCSL inflation with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations, or if adding RSAFS does not improve forecast loss versus CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS alone.
